question: Implement a `Counter` class with a private integer `value`. Overload the pre-increment (`++`) operator to increment the `value` and return the incremented object. Also, overload the post-increment (`value++`) operator.

test_cases.input: Counter c(5); std::cout << (++c).getValue() << std::endl; std::cout << c.getValue() << std::endl;
Counter c(10); std::cout << (c++).getValue() << std::endl; std::cout << c.getValue() << std::endl;
Counter c(20); Counter c_res = c++; std::cout << c.getValue() << std::endl; std::cout << c_res.getValue() << std::endl;

errors: The post-increment operator (operator++(int)) returns the incremented object (`*this`) instead of the object's state before incrementing (`temp`). This violates the expected semantics of post-increment, leading to incorrect behavior when the return value is used, effectively making it behave like pre-increment.
The robust handling of operator semantics is compromised, as the distinction between pre- and post-increment's return values is lost.
